spec_match.rationale: The simulator now models the ChromeStatus ttl extension directly: explicit ttl values, ttl=0 immediate expiry, omitted-ttl fallback, Cache-Control max-age independence, and Available-Dictionary request behavior.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome 148 exercised the TTL slider, explicit-ttl checkbox, Cache-Control slider, and age slider; each control updated the response header, request preview, dictionary state, cache state, and lifetime rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still delivers the feature-index promise and now also explains the ttl/cache distinction that the feature summary calls out.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers ttl=0, ttl shorter than Cache-Control, ttl outliving Cache-Control, omitted ttl following cache lifetime, fresh dictionaries, and evicted dictionaries.

references.rationale: The page links the ChromeStatus entry, the IETF Compression Dictionary Transport draft, and the HTTPWG ttl discussion.

design_system.rationale: Static scan found no raw hex colors, no source inline style attributes, no inline event handlers, no DOM markup assignment, and no legacy demo color aliases. Runtime interaction left document.querySelectorAll('[style]').length at 0.

summary: Repaired and rechecked with chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148. The TTL slider can now reach 0 and emits Use-As-Dictionary with ttl=0, omitted ttl falls back to HTTP cache lifetime, Cache-Control max-age is a separate live control, the page links ChromeStatus plus the IETF/HTTPWG sources, and the updated conformance route reports 7 pass / 0 fail.
